articles.title: Ethiopia Launches Major Green Energy Project
articles.description: The Ethiopian government has inaugurated a new 500 MW solar power plant in the Afar region, marking a significant step toward achieving its renewable energy targets.
key_highlights: The project is expected to provide electricity to over 2 million households.
It will reduce carbon emissions by approximately 600,000 tons annually.
Chinese and local companies collaborated on the construction.
This is part of Ethiopia’s plan to generate 100% of its electricity from renewable sources by 2030.
why_it_matters: Boosts energy security and reduces reliance on fossil fuels.
Creates thousands of jobs during construction and operation.
Positions Ethiopia as a regional leader in green energy.
Supports the country’s commitment to climate change mitigation.

articles.title: Inflation in Ethiopia Drops to Single Digits
articles.description: The National Bank of Ethiopia announced that annual inflation has fallen to 8.7%, the lowest in over three years, thanks to improved agricultural output and tighter monetary policy.
key_highlights: Food inflation decreased significantly due to good harvest season.
Central bank implemented strict liquidity management measures.
Experts predict further decline if political stability continues.
Currency exchange rate remains relatively stable this quarter.
why_it_matters: Improves purchasing power for ordinary citizens.
Creates favorable conditions for economic growth.
May lead to lower interest rates in the coming months.

articles.title: New Digital Payment System Launched in Addis Ababa
articles.description: The Ethiopian government, in partnership with private banks, has rolled out a new unified digital payment platform aimed at reducing cash dependency.
key_highlights: Users can now pay bills, transfer money, and shop using one app.
The system supports both Amharic and English interfaces.
Over 500,000 users registered in the first week.
Includes strong security features and fraud protection.
why_it_matters: Promotes financial inclusion across the country.
Reduces risks associated with carrying large amounts of cash.
Modernizes the economy and supports e-commerce growth.
Aligns Ethiopia with global digital transformation trends.
